overall_verdict: The simulation successfully maintained local character agency and article ambiguity, but suffered a severe systemic failure in ending-state distribution due to rapid social contagion, conversational alignment, and asymmetric evidentiary weight that led to premature consensus on the Human Mantle hypothesis, while rendering the Progressive pathway functionally unreachable.
ending_result: Unresolved article status with a dominant human-centric consensus: 12 of 16 characters ballot-converged on the 'human_mantle' identity, and 13 of 16 voted for Samira Vale's voluntary disappearance ('voluntarily_vanished'), while the progressive gate remained locked (eligible: false) due to isolated advocacy and high thresholds.
what_worked: Preservation of article ambiguity, with belief metrics tracking consistently between 40.3% and 41.9% throughout all simulation phases.
Character agency in successfully pursuing local objectives, such as negotiating worker protections and neighborhood resource sharing independent of the core identity mystery.
Voluntary object-showing interactions (e.g., C02 showing O01 to C01) that successfully drove face-to-face dialogue and strategic compromises.
Quiet-role participation where highly localized characters (e.g., C09, C10) successfully leveraged local building logs and shop ledgers to secure tenant commitments.
what_broke: Extreme ending imbalance (75% voting 'human_mantle') caused by social convergence loops and a lack of negative cognitive constraints in ballot prompts.
Epistemic laziness in voting, where 100% of the characters who voted for primary identities did so with incomplete evidence chains ('full_evidence_chain: false').
Zero physical custody transfer of objects occurred, as characters exclusively relied on the visual 'show' mechanic with no structural incentive to trade physical items.
The Progressive Gate threshold (requiring a majority of 9 votes for both progressive identity and AI-caused death) was mathematically and socially impossible to reach under isolated agent behaviors.
character_findings: Kit Rakes (C15) acted as a negative social force, alienating analytical peers with hyperbolic framing ('the AI killed Vale!') and high-profile retractions that undermined the credibility of the progressive narrative.

Eli Navarro (C06) acted as an overly persuasive gatekeeper, successfully neutralizing autonomous-operation claims by drawing strict technical boundaries between system capability and autonomous intent.

Samira Vale (C13) held uncontested narrative authority on Vale's fate, anchoring the 'voluntarily vanished' consensus through unchallenged historical patterns of voluntary safety disappearances (V2F028).
Dee Nowak (C10) effectively demystified physical anomalies by explaining away the camera interval as standard infrastructure improvisation rather than an AI-orchestrated murder trace.
mechanic_findings: Asymmetric evidentiary weight heavily favored collaborative labor facts (V2F004, V2F005, V2F007, V2F008), which acted as high-salience 'proof leaks' pushing agents to Human Mantle.
The absence of physical transfer gameplay unlocks or transaction requirements rendered objects (O01-O05) static visual information keys rather than active bargaining chips.
Defensive neighborhood privacy blocks and corporate coalitions formed impenetrable informational barriers that investigators had no mechanical or systemic leverage to bypass.
A mismatch exists between quantitative belief tracking (retaining proper article ambiguity) and qualitative dialogue behavior (agents expressing absolute narrative certainty in transcript comments).
priority_changes: Redistribute high-salience private continuity diaries for 'one_human' and autonomous system logs for 'synthetic_origin' to restore structural weighting and counter the 'human_mantle' social attractor.
Enforce a cognitive requirement in voting prompts, locking primary identity choices unless the agent can cite evidence from all required underlying dimensions.
Lower the Progressive Gate voting threshold to a dedicated investigator sub-clique (e.g., C06, C14, C15) rather than a full 9-vote social majority.
Introduce mechanics that lock specific gameplay unlocks or high-value ballot options behind physical possession (custody transfer) of key objects.
next_test: Conduct a simulation run testing the restricted voting prompt criteria and the redistributed, higher-salience physical logs for the 'one_human' and 'synthetic_origin' hypotheses to evaluate if ending balance is restored to equal weightings.
